ogTitle: Tennyson Point vs Tenterfield: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Tennyson Point and Tenterfield.
heading: Tennyson Point vs Tenterfield — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tennyson Point, New South Wales and Tenterfield, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tenterfield has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Tennyson Point (48/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tennyson Point or Tenterfield safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tennyson Point has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Tenterfield scores 47/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tennyson Point scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. Tenterfield scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tennyson Point or Tenterfield?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tenterfield has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
